name: Valentine Surprise
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the cake according to package directions using a greased and floured 9-inch heart-shaped pan at least 3 inches deep. Alternatively, bake in two layer pans.
2. Remove from pans and transfer to a wire rack; let cool.
3. For the filling: In a saucepan, combine sugar, corn syrup, and water. Bring to a rolling boil over medium heat. Cover and continue boiling until the mixture reaches 242°F on a candy thermometer.
4. Meanwhile, beat egg whites until stiff. Slowly add the hot syrup mixture to the egg whites while beating at medium speed with an electric mixer.
5. Add vanilla and continue beating at high speed until stiff peaks form. Fold in strawberries (optional).
6. If the cake has been baked in layers, stack the layers and slice off approximately 1/2 inch from the top of each layer. Reserve the tops for later use.
7. Scoop out the center of each cake layer to within 1 inch of the sides. Fill the hollowed-out cakes with filling. Replace the reserved tops on the cakes.
8. For the glaze, combine chocolate morsels, shortening, corn syrup, and water in a double boiler over boiling water until melted and stirring frequently.
9. With the assembled cake still on the wire rack, slowly pour the glaze over the cake in a circular motion, smoothing it onto the sides as it drips down with a small metal spatula or knife.
10. Refrigerate until set. Store in the refrigerator.
Note: The cake will appear shiny and chocolatey until it is cut!
